summary: End-to-end testing of the new AI Coach feature (backend + UI). 7/7 new backend pytest cases pass (coach settings seeding, reminders auto-seed, PUT settings persistence, /coach/today shape, /coach/tip real LLM, /api/reminders full CRUD, /api/ai/discord/status idle). UI verified on live preview URL: login -> Sidebar 'AI Coach' nav -> /coach page renders Discord 'Not Connected' card, Today plan (Tuesday 09 June 2026 + workout focus 'Legs Day — Squats, Lunges, Calf raises, Wall sit (3-4 sets)' + 3 seeded reminders), Schedule & Timing editor, Weekly Home Workout Split editor, and Reminders list (3 seeded). Briefing time 06:30 persisted across reload (GET /api/coach/settings), briefing-toggle off persisted across reload, plan-mon edit persisted across reload, Add Reminder modal creates row, Edit Reminder updates time to 20:30 in list. Real-LLM /api/coach/tip returns ~378 char coaching note. Discord bot endpoint correctly reports configured:false (idle).

ui_bugs.issue: Delete icon button uses `opacity-40 group-hover:opacity-100`, so without hover it is semi-transparent and harder to click in automated tests. force=True click on `[data-testid="delete-item"]` did not consistently open the AlertDialog in one of two automation runs (confirm-delete element never appeared, 30s timeout). Real users hovering see the full button, so functional impact is small, but consider always-on icons OR a higher base opacity (e.g., opacity-60 -> opacity-100) for reliability and accessibility.

issues: Reminders modal opens fine but the Title <input> has no `name`/`id` attribute on the underlying input, only an accessible label. data-testid on each form field inside CrudModal would make automation simpler and more robust.

action_items: (Optional, LOW) Increase base opacity of edit/delete icons on reminder rows so the delete confirm flow is reliable without hover (also better for touch/keyboard users).
(Optional, design) Add `name` attribute or `data-testid` to inputs rendered inside CrudModal so test scripts can target Title/Time/Days/Message fields directly instead of by ordinal position.

(Pre-existing, from iter_2) Move ai-launcher to bottom-24 right-6 (already noted as moved per problem statement) and keep clear of 'Made with Emergent' badge — verified the launcher icon now sits well above the badge in screenshots.
critical_code_review_comments: coach.py get_settings(): the merge `merged = {...DEFAULT_SETTINGS, **{k: doc.get(k, ...)}}` ignores any top-level keys returned by `doc` that are not in DEFAULT_SETTINGS (e.g. created_at, updated_at). Harmless but `user_id`/timestamps never reach the client — intentional; fine.

coach.py reminders_today(): the day filter logic `days == 'daily' or (isinstance(days,str) and (days=='' or wk in days))` treats any string containing the weekday substring as a match (e.g., days='monday' matches 'mon'). Acceptable for current UI (which only emits exact tokens), but consider exact-token matching via split(',') to be safer if a user types free-form.
coach.py: `_now()` returns naive UTC isoformat (`datetime.utcnow().isoformat()`) while server.py uses tz-aware iso. Inconsistent — pick one to avoid future parse bugs. Not blocking.
coach.py uses a SEPARATE module-level Mongo client (`_db` from ai_assistant) on top of server.py's `db`. Two connection pools to the same DB; functional but wasteful. Centralize a single client.
server.py is now ~728 lines (still over 700 threshold). Splitting into routers (auth/crud/aggregations/ai/coach) recommended.

Coach.jsx: `saveSettings` POSTs the WHOLE settings object every time including workout_plan — backend merges with DEFAULT_PLAN so a missing day reverts to default. Acceptable, but a delta-only PUT would be cleaner.

Coach.jsx CrudModal usage: REMINDER_FIELDS uses select for `active` with string 'true'/'false', then handleReminderSubmit coerces to boolean. Works correctly (verified via backend), but a boolean Switch in the modal would be a cleaner UX.
Coach.jsx: useResource('reminders') re-fetches on every Coach page mount — already covered by react-query caching, no issue.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on required Coach flows verified end-to-end. The delete-via-UI confirm step was flaky in one automation run due to low-opacity icon; backend DELETE /api/reminders/{id} verified working both via pytest and via direct API cleanup.

seed_data_creation: On first GET /api/coach/settings, backend seeded 3 starter reminders (Home Workout 18:00, Take Medicine 21:00, Drink Water 12:00) — intentional product behavior. Tests created/cleaned up: 1x TEST_Auto_Reminder reminder (cleaned via API), briefing_time/plan-mon temporarily changed and restored to defaults (07:00 briefing, default Monday Push Day text).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Coach backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_coach.py (pytest, ~10s; /api/coach/tip is a real LLM call). Use ibne@rihat.universe / Dominate2026 for owner login. Coach.jsx CrudModal inputs do not have name/id attrs — target by ordinal: page.locator('[role="dialog"] input').nth(0)=title, nth(1)=time. Reminder row edit/delete buttons use opacity-40 group-hover:opacity-100 — when automating delete, hover the row first OR rely on JS .click(). Discord bot remains idle (configured:false). The page route is /coach and the sidebar testid is nav-coach. The 3 default reminders are seeded once on first /coach/settings GET per user; subsequent reads do not re-seed.
rca of the issue: Flaky UI delete: the delete icon button is rendered with className containing `opacity-40 group-hover:opacity-100`. In automated Playwright runs without a real hover (and with the row's `group` class not always triggering hover state under force=True clicks), the icon stayed at opacity 40%. Playwright's force=True clicks past visibility but if the synthetic pointer event reaches the parent row first, setDeleting(r) never fires and the AlertDialog with [data-testid="confirm-delete"] is never mounted. Mitigation: bump base opacity OR have the row attach the `hover` group state earlier OR add a separate always-visible 'overflow' menu trigger. Functionally the DELETE endpoint works perfectly (verified by pytest + API cleanup).
